meaning: adj.软弱的,无活力的,没气力的
examples: As of 21 April 2010, 120 cases of acute flaccid paralysis had been reported from Tajikistan.
In 2009, Tajikistan reported 35 cases of acute flaccid paralysis, of which none were caused by wild poliovirus.
My student tried hard to get the thick furred look of the blue area, but all he could manage was a flaccid, watery pool of blue.
Surveillance is rigorous - 35, 325 reporting sites across the country are active and pick up cases of Acute Flaccid Paralysis.
Perhaps it was nothing more than an effect of the light, but his face looked heartbreakingly flaccid, white like the underside of a crab.
